summary: Phase 11 — booking-confirmed and soft-lock-reminder notification triggers tested end-to-end. 7/7 new pytest cases pass plus 64/64 regression cases (Phase 6/7/9/10 + booking_id uniqueness). Verified via DB row counts, channel coverage (sms/whatsapp/in_app), and supervisor log markers.
critical_code_review_comments: schedule_soft_lock_reminder() relies on asyncio.create_task in the running FastAPI loop — fine for live server, but the task is process-local: a worker restart while a soft-lock is pending will silently drop the reminder. Consider a persistent scheduler (APScheduler with Mongo store or Celery beat) for production.

notify_host_booking_confirmed swallows all exceptions with logger.error only — no retry / dead-letter. Acceptable for demo (mocked transports) but should integrate with a real DLQ before live MSG91/SendGrid are wired.
send_multi_channel_notification helper does not return the inserted notification_ids; tests had to query Mongo directly. Returning the persisted rows would simplify both tests and audit.

booking_routes.py is now 548 lines; create_booking + confirm_payment + mock_pay each share ~30 lines of post-confirm side-effect logic (blocked_dates upsert + notify task). Worth extracting to a single _finalize_confirmed_booking(db, booking_dict) helper.
Two `import asyncio` statements occur inside route handlers (lines 257, 536). Move to module top-level imports.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing demo properties prop_demo_1..5_1778066214 with deep-future windows (offset >= 1500 days) to avoid 409s.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 11 is fully wired and verified. Tests use module-scope tokens but FUNCTION-scope motor client (event-loop closes between async tests under pytest-asyncio mode=auto). For reminder behaviour the test invokes services.booking_notifications._soft_lock_reminder_task directly with delay=1 — no real 8-minute wait. Note: when invoked in the test process the [DEMO]/[MOCK EMAIL] log lines go to pytest stderr, NOT /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log; DB row counts are the authoritative assertion. Regression suite installed pytest-asyncio==1.3.0.
